guideline: Always separate product catalog, cart, checkout, payment gateway, shipping rules, and admin order management in scope.
Authentication, gated content, subscriptions, cancellation, and member dashboard should be listed explicitly.
Clarify whether bookings are requests, confirmed appointments, calendar sync, or paid reservations.
Separate SaaS scope into auth, roles, dashboard, core workflow, data model, admin tools, exports/imports, integrations, deployment, QA, and post-launch support.
For parser tools, explicitly scope supported file types, OCR vs text extraction, AI/API usage, accuracy target, review UI, validation rules, export formats, and edge-case handling.
State whether the quote covers iOS, Android, or both, and whether backend/API work is included.
Phase social apps into MVP feed/profile/media/moderation first, and exclude advanced recommendations, filters, stories, livestreaming, or creator analytics unless confirmed.

Phase mobile games around a fixed core loop, platform target, art/audio ownership, level or content count, leaderboard needs, monetization hooks, app-store QA, and explicit exclusions for multiplayer, 3D, live ops, and advanced economy systems.
Separate wireframes, high-fidelity screens, prototype, design system, and developer handoff.
Clarify research depth, SEO keywords, word count, revision rounds, and CMS upload responsibility.
Separate data cleanup, source connection, KPI definitions, dashboard pages, refresh automation, and handoff.
Every fixed-price scope should include revision limits and explicit out-of-scope items.
